content:
⚖️ INDIAN JUDICIAL BENCHES EXPLAINED
Composition • Powers • Functions • Precedent Hierarchy

🔹 Key Highlights from the Indian Judicial System:

✅ Single Bench (1 Judge)
• Handles routine matters, writs, bail applications & procedural hearings
• Commonly seen in High Courts
• Cannot overrule larger benches

✅ Division Bench (2 Judges)
• Deals with appeals, important constitutional & criminal matters
• Can refer conflicting issues to a larger bench
• Common in High Courts & Supreme Court

✅ Full Bench (3+ Judges)
• Constituted to resolve conflicting judgments
• Settles important legal questions
• Holds stronger precedential value

✅ Constitution Bench (5+ Judges)
• Formed under Article 145(3) of the Constitution
• Decides substantial constitutional questions
• Landmark constitutional interpretations emerge from here

✅ Larger Bench Principle
📌 “Only a bench of greater strength can overrule a smaller bench.”
This principle maintains judicial discipline and consistency in precedents.

📚 Whether you're preparing for:
• Judiciary Exams
• CLAT PG / UGC NET Law
• UPSC Law Optional
• Law School Internals
• Moot Courts & Interviews
